pain_point_label: Weak Integration with Core Curriculum & Hidden Learning Outcomes

matches.match_rationale: The absence of a co-curricular curriculum map and competency framework is a document creation problem. AI can draft the framework itself — mapping co-curricular activities to 21st-century competency standards — and generate activity-specific learning outcome statements that teachers can review and adopt. This directly attacks the root cause: no articulated connection between enrichment activities and academic competencies.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A generator that takes a co-curricular activity description and outputs a structured learning outcome narrative aligned to school competency frameworks, suitable for inclusion in student portfolios or teacher lesson notes.

matches.match_rationale: The core problem is that relationships between co-curricular activities and academic competencies are invisible — they exist implicitly but are never articulated. Knowledge extraction can parse existing activity logs, student records, club descriptions, and curriculum documents to surface latent competency connections (e.g., robotics club → engineering design process → STEM standards). This creates the structural backbone of a co-curricular curriculum map without requiring staff to build it manually.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An extraction pipeline that ingests co-curricular program descriptions and academic standards documents, outputs a relationship graph linking activities to competencies, which can seed a living curriculum map dashboard for coordinators.

matches.match_rationale: Staff don't see co-curricular as core to learning because they lack easy access to evidence and precedents linking enrichment to outcomes. A RAG system over internal activity records, student performance data, and external competency frameworks lets teachers query 'which clubs develop the communication skills I need for this unit?' and get grounded, citation-backed answers. This converts invisible curriculum connections into an on-demand staff resource.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A staff-facing Q&A tool that answers questions like 'Which students in my class have co-curricular experience relevant to this project unit?' by retrieving and synthesizing from student activity logs and curriculum standards.

matches.match_rationale: Building and maintaining a co-curricular curriculum map requires ongoing multi-step work: collecting activity data from club coordinators, mapping competencies, updating records when programs change, and notifying relevant teachers. A task automation agent can orchestrate this workflow — pulling activity submissions, running them through a competency-mapping model, updating a shared curriculum map, and triggering notifications to teachers whose subject areas are touched. This removes the human coordination overhead that currently makes the map nonexistent.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An automated pipeline triggered each term: collects co-curricular activity reports, maps them to curriculum competencies, updates a central map, and sends each teacher a digest of co-curricular activity relevant to their subject — with zero manual coordination.
